<!--
Thank you for contributing to Framecheck.

Two house rules before you submit:

  1. DCO sign-off. Every commit must carry a `Signed-off-by:` trailer.
     The DCO is checked automatically; failures block merge. See
     CONTRIBUTING.md for the full contract and `git commit -s`
     workflow.

  2. Tests must pass. Run `python3 run_tests.py` locally before
     pushing. CI runs the same suite.
-->

## Summary
<!-- One or two sentences. What does this PR change, and why. -->

## Change scope
<!-- Check what applies. Leave the rest. -->
- [ ] Bug fix (no behavior change beyond the fix)
- [ ] New feature (user-visible behavior changes)
- [ ] Refactor (internal restructuring; no behavior change)
- [ ] Documentation only (no code change)
- [ ] Build / CI / tooling (no production behavior change)
- [ ] Frame Vocabulary Standard catalog (`data/frame_library/`,
      `data/frame_library_v3/`)
- [ ] MCP server surface (`mcp_server.py`, related modules)
- [ ] Other:

## Test plan
<!-- How you verified the change. Be specific.
     Example: "Added test_frame_X.py case Y; ran `python3 run_tests.py`
     locally; confirmed prior 49/49 suites still pass plus the new one."
-->

## Documentation touched
<!-- List any .md / NOTICE / SECURITY / CHANGELOG / README updates,
     or "none". For user-visible changes, CHANGELOG.md should usually
     gain an entry under [Unreleased].
-->

## DCO sign-off
- [ ] All commits in this PR carry `Signed-off-by:` (per CONTRIBUTING.md
      and the DCO check workflow).
